_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- determine the most appropriate technologies to achieve the organisation’s objectives
- assess the effect of changes on different parts of the organisation and its partners
- identify underlying causes of technology challenges faced by other leaders and make strategic recommendations to resolve them
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- inspiring people with your vision so they support a more effective technology strategy
- helping other leaders prioritise a strategic technical direction over immediate tactical concerns
- educating colleagues about the potential benefits of chosen technologies
- earning the trust of stakeholders who are invested in specific technologies so that they agree to change
reference_document_element_description: Ability to synthesize market trends, competitive intelligence, technology evolution, and organizational capability into coherent strategic recommendations that inform executive decision-making.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-A-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-A-003 (LS, cosine: 0.493), LS-T-004 (LS, cosine: 0.481).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.562). Shared vocabulary: [recommendations, strategic, technology]. DDaT-only terms [achieve, agree, appropriate, assess, benefits, causes] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, capability, coherent, competitive, decision, evolution] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.840 (P=0.821, R=0.859). R exceeds P by 0.038 — the WIDAI KSA's 20 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 107 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (95.1%). "You can:
- determine the most appropriate technologies to achieve the organisati" and "Ability to synthesize market trends, competitive intelligence, technology evolut" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.465 < STS 0.562 (gap -0.097). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [achieve, agree, appropriate] and [ability, capability, coherent] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.5%. Shared tokens: [recommendations, strategic, technology]. DDaT-only: [achieve, agree, appropriate, assess, benefits, causes]. WIDAI-only: [ability, capability, coherent, competitive, decision, evolution].
